name: Blue Cheese Columbine Dip
directions: Set blue cheese aside.
Combine remaining ingredients in a bowl.
Add blue cheese.
Cover and let stand at room temperature 2 hours.
Serve on crackers.

name: Cream Dip for Fruit
directions: Blend all the ingredients together, and serve with a fresh fruit platter.

name: Creamy Herb Dip
directions: Put cream cheese in small bowl and gradually add buttermilk until smooth.
Stir in remaining ingredients.

name: Cream Cheese and Jalapeno Tortillas
directions: Mix together jalapenos, turkey, onion, and seasonings.  Add serrano pepper, if you want these spicy.
Blend in softened cream cheese, add jalapeno juice to thin cream cheese.
Spread the mixture in a thin layer on each tortilla.
Roll up tortillas.
Place rolled up tortillas back into the plastic tortilla bag.
Secure bag with a twisty tie.
Refrigerate overnight.
Slice rolls into 1/4 inch pieces and you are ready to serve.
The rolls can be frozen and sliced before serving.

name: Garlicky Parsley Mayonnaise
directions: In a blender, mix everything except cream cheese until emulsified.
Pour mixtrue into a bowl and stir in cream cheese.
Check seasoning.
Refrigerate for 1-2 hour.
add chopped parsley before serving.

name: Cottage Salad
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl.
Line plates with lettuce.
Scoop the salad onto the lettuce.

name: Fresh Basil Cheese Dip
directions: In a blender or food processorr combine cottage cheese, basil,garlic, Parmesan cheese, salt and pepper.
Process for 1 to 2 minutes, stopping machine and scraping down side of container one or twice.
Place dip into small dish.

name: Backyard Taco Dip
directions: Ensure cream cheese is at room temperature.
Whip in a large bowl until smooth.
Mix in salsa.
Add in peppers and cheddar.
Serve with taco chips.

name: Marinated Cheese Appetizer
directions: Cut cheddar cheese and cream cheese into 1/4 inch slices, then again in half.
Using a small dish, place cheese slices on end alternating cheddar and cream cheese.
Make the marinade by combining sugar, basil, salt, pepper, oil, vinegar, pimento, parsley, onion and garlic.
Pour marinade over cheese, cover and refrigerate overnight.
Serve with crackers.

name: Classic Cheese Log
directions: In a large bowl, bring cheese to room temperature.
Add sour cream, horseradish mustard, and onion powder.
Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until combined and slightly fluffy.
Line a 4 cup mold or a 7X3-inch loaf pan with plastic wrap.
Sprinkle half the pecans into the mold.
Spread half the cheese mixture over the pecans.
Top with remaining pecans.
Then, spread the remaining cheese mixture on top.
Press into pan to make a even layer.
Cover and chill for 2-48 hours.
To serve, unmold on a serving tray and let stand 10 minutes before serving.

name: Cheese'n Onion Dip
directions: In a blender or food processor, cream cottage cheese and yogurt until smooth.
Add remaining ingredients, cover.
Refrigerate to blend flavours.
Serve with raw vegetables.

name: Center Piece Cheese Mix
directions: Mix cheeses together in bowl,using mixer.
Add pickles and onion.
Shape into ring or ball.
Press peppers into cheese to help them stick.
Chill.

name: Pimiento Cheese Dip
directions: Place ingredients into blender container.
Process until smooth and creamy.

name: Cream Cheese Sardine Dip
directions: Place all ingredients in food processor and blend till smooth.
Serve on raw veggies, crackers or Melba toast.

name: Seafood Surprise Dip
directions: Mix cream cheese, sour cream and mayonnaise and spread over 12" pan or plate.
Scatter shrimp/crab over cheese mixture, add seafood sauce, then remaining ingredients.
Chill until ready to serve.

name: Cheese Ball Maximus
directions: The key to this cheese ball is the prep.
After finely chopping each item, please blot well with paper towel.
You must do this or you will have a cheese dip.
Pay close attention to blotting the cherries or you will have very pink cheese ball.
Mix well and form into a ball and chill overnight.
Roll in chopped pecans if desired.
This is the best cheese ball ever, promise.

name: Creamy Blue Cheese Dip
directions: Combine the cream cheese, blue cheese, butter, sour cream, and garlic in a food processor or blender and process or blend until smooth.
With machine on, pour in the cream and blend well.
Transfer to a serving bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Makes 32 servings (2 cups).
Make-Ahead Tip: Prepare as directed above.
Cover and chill up to 48 hours.

name: Mamma's Shrimp Dip
directions: Spray dish with Pam and spread the softened cream cheese in layer.
Pour jar Picante Sauce over cream cheese.
Sprinkle shrimp on top of Picante Sauce.
Let sit in refrigerator over night.
Take out and let set for a while before serving to allow cream cheese to soften.
Serve with assorted crackers.

name: Feta Cheese And Fig Salsa
directions: Combine all the ingredients together in a bowl.
Mix well.
Serve.

name: Fresh Fruit Dip
directions: Combine softened cream cheese and marshmallow cream until well blended.
Add orange juice and mix well.
Serve with fresh fruit.

name: Homemade Velveeta - Substitute / Copycat
directions: Line a 8x4x2 - 5" loaf pan with plastic wrap.
In a blender, put 1/2 cup of the water, 3 tablespoons of the milk, 1/2 teaspoon of the gelatin.  Whip until gelatin is dissolved.
Quickly add 1/2 lb of the cheddar to the hot mixture.
Whip until blended.
Pour into the prepared loaf pan.
Repeat this twice, until all ingredients are used.
Cover the pan with more plastic wrap and chill overnight before unmolding.
Keep cold and slice as needed.

name: Ketchup Dip
directions: Soften cream cheese in a bowl.
Add Ketchup.
Mix until pink, add more ketchup to taste.

name: Bacon Ranch Cheddar Dip
directions: In a small bowl, mix the dip mix with the sour cream and mayonnaise.
Add the remaining ingredients, and mix until combined.
Chill before serving.

name: Layered Salmon and Avocado Dip
directions: Using an electric mixer-beat the cream cheese until smooth.
Stir in the remaining 'salmon layer' ingredients.
Place the ring moulds onto a plate. Spoon the salmon mixture into the bottom of the moulds. Use a spoon to smooth the surface over. Chill while you make the next layer.

To make the avocado layer- combine all avocado layer ingredients and season with salt and pepper to taste. Layer over the salmon mixture and top with herbs of your choice. Chill another 30 minutes or until required.
Carefully remove rings to serve. You can cut them with scissors to make the removal easier.
Serve with crackers of your choice. Enjoy!

name: Thyme and Garlic Cheese Dip
directions: Blend first four ingredients in food processor until garlic is finely chopped, occasionally scraping down sides of bowl.
Add cheese and sour cream; blend well.
Season with more salt and pepper if desired.
Transfer to serving bowl; cover and chill in the refrigerator 30 minutes. (Dip can be made on day ahead. Cover and chill.).

name: Marinated Cheese
directions: Combine all ingredients, mixing gently, and let sit in the refrigerator overnight. Gently turn or mix a couple of times. Serve on platter and drizzle extra olive oil over.

name: Amber Glow Dip (Cream Cheese, Apricot and Horseradish Plus)
directions: In a small bowl, mix everything but the cream cheese and the nuts.
Place the cheese on a plate and top with the apricot mixture.
Sprinkle with chopped peanuts, if you like, and serve with crackers.

name: Boomette's Smoked Oysters Appetizers
directions: Put the whipped onion cream cheese on each Ritz crackers then top with a smoked oyster.  And enjoy!

name: Blue Cheese Dip
directions: Mix together all dip ingredients in a bowl (for a smoother consistency use a food processor).
The dip shouldn’t be perfectly smooth like sour cream; it should have some texture.
I mash the blue cheese and mix the dip by hand, but it will be chunky.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
The dip keeps in the fridge for about two days.

name: Salsa Roll-Ups
directions: Mix cream cheese and salsa; spread onto tortillas. Top with remaining ingredients.
Roll up tortillas tightly. Cut each crosswise into 5 slices.

name: Kimchi Pimento Cheese
directions: Beat the cream cheese in a mixer with the paddle attachment until smooth and slightly aerated.

Meanwhile, squeeze the liquid out of the kimchi with your hands (or in a sieve with the back of a wooden spoon) and reserve it for the next step. Pulse the kimchi several times in a food processor, or chop it finely.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the Tabasco, mayonnaise, vinegar, and 2 Tablespoons of the kimchi liquid.
Using a rubber spatula, scrape down the sides of the bowl. Turn the mixer on to medium, add the cheddar, and mix for one minute, then stop, scrape again, and mix for another minute.
Without stopping the mixer, drizzle the liquid ingredients into the cheese mixture and continue to mix until completely incorporated.
Remove the bowl from the stand and fold in the pimentos and chopped kimchi.
Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 12 hours.
Allow to rest out of the refrigerator for an hour before serving.
Serve with your favorite potato chips or chicharrones.
